key_facts: Dashboard tracks Taostats subnet 110 using plain JavaScript Node.js and local SQLite.
Live poll intervals are limited to 60/120/240 minutes, persisted in SQLite app_settings, and can be changed from the admin drawer when TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured.

The top bar shows the current TAO price used for USD conversion, the next scheduled poll time as a local timestamp, and a wallet activity cache badge with cached row count, last sync time, and next sync time.
The dashboard now opens with a wallet section first, followed by a collapsible financial perspective panel that explains price, flow, sentiment, and supply pressure in plain language.
The dashboard also includes a 'What matters most today' panel that highlights the main correlation points for beginners.
The financial perspective area now also includes a watchlist panel that keeps the core alerts (price vs flow, sentiment watch, supply watch) visible in a stable beginner-friendly format.

The dashboard can now track multiple configured wallet balances from Taostats account latest/history endpoints using coldkeys, optional hotkeys, optional hotkey ROLE tags for validator/owner attribution, and wallet names from .env, and the wallet modal now also shows a compact wallet profile with rank, created-on date, hotkey summary, estimated income sources, current subnet stake positions, and a collapsible hotkey history section backed by historical stake snapshots with delta color-coding.

The subnet stats section now includes an Alpha Holders card that derives the live holder-row count from the local alpha_holder_snapshots table, stores the latest holder addresses from Taostats stake-balance data, opens a historical trend from the locally stored snapshot history, is fed by a dedicated daily alpha-holder snapshot job at UTC midnight, and keeps the Alpha holder addresses section collapsed by default.

The app now keeps a local subnet metadata cache so alpha-holder ranking, rank-history, and subnet header labels can show friendly names like Chutes (SN64) even when the current subnet snapshot row is missing.

A dedicated subnet-name-backfill command now refreshes the local subnet metadata cache by pulling the Taostats subnet catalog and falling back to the latest subnet snapshot when a catalog row is unnamed.
The subnet-name-backfill command now uses a small concurrent worker queue and reports skip reasons so unnamed or invalid catalog rows are easier to diagnose during refreshes.

The alpha-holder ranking view now collapses into a current-subnet rank card by default and expands into a table-first leaderboard with rank, subnet, alpha-holder count, daily change, and a tiny trend sparkline, while still using the latest stored subnet name when available so labels read like Chutes (SN64) instead of plain SN64.

The dashboard now also includes an all-subnet alpha-holder ranking panel that compares every locally stored subnet by latest holder-row count, highlights SN110 in the table, and now presents the ranking as a collapsible current-subnet card that expands into the full table built from the same local alpha_holder_snapshots table.

The alpha-holder CLI commands (--alpha-holder-backfill / --alpha-holder-history-backfill and --alpha-holder-sync) print live per-subnet progress, worker ids, elapsed time, remaining counts, and an ETA to stderr so long fills are observable in terminal runs.

While an alpha-holder backfill is running, the app stores a local pause flag in SQLite app_settings so scheduled polling, wallet sync, and scheduled alpha-holder snapshots skip their Taostats requests instead of competing with the manual fill.

Alpha-holder backfill now also emits per-page heartbeat lines while each subnet's stake-balance snapshot fetch is in flight, and the manual backfill path uses a small 3-worker subnet queue while the scheduled sync stays sequential; the alpha-holder snapshot fetch path now also keeps a slightly higher retry budget for 429s so a transient Taostats throttle can be retried more than once before the run fails.

The stake-balance latest fetcher now clamps its page size to 200 and continues paging using that actual page size, because Taostats returns capped pages and the previous limit comparison stopped after the first page for most subnets.

If stake-balance latest returns HTTP 429 during alpha-holder backfill, the fetcher pauses for roughly a minute (or longer if Retry-After says so), emits a retry-wait message, and now retries the page more than once before surfacing the error.
The alpha-holder-history-backfill alias now reuses the supported snapshot backfill path because Taostats does not expose a stable subnet-history endpoint for holders.
The package scripts now include npm run alpha-holder-history-backfill and npm run alpha-holder-sync as aliases for the existing alpha-holder CLI flags.

The wallet modal previously regressed because renderWalletDetails referenced a nonexistent walletStakeHistory variable; keep the browser modal using state.modalStakeHistory and guard wallet-specific rendering so a hotkey-history issue cannot blank the main wallet chart.

The dashboard now includes a standalone Pool growth estimator section directly underneath the wallet section; it uses the latest local subnet pool snapshot, simulates TAO injection locally, and must fail closed if the pool reserves or price are missing.

The pool growth estimator now also shows implied projected subnet market cap plus reserve impact alongside the alpha and price estimates; those market-cap figures are derived from the current snapshot and should be treated as estimates.

The pool growth estimator also includes a collapsible compact scenario curve for projected alpha price change versus TAO injected across a fixed 0–2,500 TAO range; when the curve is collapsed, the cards reflow to fill the panel, and when expanded the curve returns beside the stacked result cards on wide screens with a smooth layout transition, hover crosshairs, tooltip readouts, and no point markers on the line for precise inspection.
The scenario chart toggle should render as a mixed-case Show chart / Hide chart pill rather than all caps, so the control reads naturally alongside the rest of the estimator panel.

The pool growth estimator source lives in src/pool-estimator.js and the behavior should stay documented in docs/pool-growth-estimator.md so future edits keep the local constant-product approximation, the implied market-cap output, the fixed-range scenario curve, the hover interaction, and the unavailable-state behavior aligned.

Ctrl/Cmd-clicking a wallet card now opens a wallet transaction modal that combines Taostats extrinsics, transfers, and hotkey stake deltas so the user can inspect the activity behind a wallet change, and the modal reads from the local SQLite cache first with a visible 'Fetching wallet activity…' loading state before falling back to Taostats on cache misses or manual refresh.
The TAO price badge opens a historical TAO/USD modal chart.

Historical metric modals support 24H, 7D, 14D, 30D, and 60D windows, default to 7D on open, include beginner-friendly metric explanations, and can slide by 24 hours with arrow buttons or keyboard shortcuts.

The main dashboard keeps the Latest JSON, History JSON, Recent snapshots, and Latest ingest run inside a collapsible admin panel, and that admin panel is only rendered when TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured.

When TAOSTATS_ADMIN_API_KEY is configured, the POST admin routes (/api/subnets/:netuid/ingest, /api/subnets/:netuid/backfill, and /api/settings/poll-interval) must receive the matching X-Admin-API-Key header; the browser admin controls already send it.

The hidden admin drawer now includes a schedules-and-runs table that shows the configured cadence, next run, last recorded result, and error text for subnet polling, wallet sync, and the alpha-holder snapshot job; it also surfaces a pause notice while alpha-holder backfill is active.
The admin panel also exposes a browser-triggered backfill form that posts to /api/subnets/:netuid/backfill, plus a wallet activity backfill control that posts to /api/subnets/:netuid/wallet-backfill.

Backfill mode imports subnet history, TAO price history, Tao Flow history, configured wallet balance history, historical hotkey stake snapshots, and alpha-holder address snapshots, then refreshes the live snapshot.

A dedicated alpha-holder backfill command can snapshot the current holder rows for every discovered subnet so the ranking and SN110 rank-history views start from a local baseline, and the daily UTC snapshot job keeps those all-subnet samples growing after the initial collection starts.

Wallet activity uses a dedicated SQLite cache table for extrinsics, transfers, and derived stake-delta events, a separate wallet-backfill command, and a rolling scheduled sync window so overlapping runs dedupe safely.
TAO-denominated values can be toggled between TAO and USD using the stored TAO price history.
Missing metric values must remain null in charts and render as gaps, not zeroes.

Current hotkey stake positions are fetched live, and the wallet modal can now show an estimated income-sources section that splits wallet growth into validator/owner buckets for known hotkeys while leaving any untagged remainder in the residual/unclassified bucket.
Subnet stats use a 4-column grid, and collapsible panels use a visible chevron affordance.

Taomarketcap's public subnet table endpoint exposes a cached chain_buys_per_block field, so the repo can source the homepage Chain Buys value locally once it is wired into the cached subnet snapshot model.

Taostats wallet/API calls are currently blocked by 429 "Insufficient credits (remaining: 0, required: 1)" responses, so wallet snapshots, stake-balance, and extrinsic requests cannot advance until credits are replenished.
The subnet polling path is scrape-based and separate from the Taostats API wallet path; the wallet data staleness is not caused by the scrape-based subnet source.
Both TAOSTATS_API_KEY and TAOSTATS_API_KEY2 currently hit the same Taostats insufficient-credits response.
The wallet ingest path now short-circuits when the snapshot call reports exhausted credits and backs off for hours instead of retrying every minute.
The experimental UI now uses a tiered card system: compact for lightweight metrics, deep for richer summary cards, and entity for wallets.

In experimental mode, the wallet cards keep the holographic entity treatment, the overview/signal cards use the deep variant, the subnet stats and 24h comparison cards now use the same deep styling, and the financial perspective/watchlist/admin ingest surfaces also follow the tiered card language for consistency.
The experimental 'What changed in the last 24h' section now renders as a 4-column by 2-row grid on desktop and collapses cleanly on mobile.
The experimental drawer content now collapses to a single column on mobile, and the wallet entity cards use a mobile-specific internal layout so their stats do not overflow the viewport.

The experimental dashboard will gain a local-only customization mode in the browser: users can reorder cards within a section, hide/show specific cards, and reset the layout with preferences saved to localStorage so the stable dashboard and SQLite data model stay untouched.

The experimental dashboard now supports a local-only Customize mode in the browser with per-section card reordering, hide/show controls, and a Reset layout action, all persisted in localStorage without changing the stable dashboard.

The experimental dashboard customization mode now uses the original FLIP-style reflow with drag-over-before/after cues for actual card moves, and that is the preferred reorder motion in the experimental grid while still honoring prefers-reduced-motion.

In experimental mode, the wallet cards were moved out of the Explore More drawer and promoted above Key Metrics; the wallet card now shows the raw alpha value as the primary alpha figure with the TAO equivalent underneath, the 24h wallet change pill also shows a percent, the key metrics and subnet stats cards now show a subtle wallet-like 24h status chip inline with the main value, the token price chip keeps precise TAO decimals and a percent delta so tiny moves do not collapse to zero, and deep cards without a delta keep their prime value aligned to the top instead of bottom-aligning it.
